preamble: Voicing assimilation is a common phonetic process where a voiceless sound becomes voiced due to the influence of neighboring voiced sounds. This phenomenon can affect retroflex stops, transforming the voiceless retroflex stop /ʈ/ into its voiced counterpart /ɖ/. This shift is often triggered by a voiced phonetic environment, such as being intervocalic or adjacent to a nasal consonant.

examples.note: In Kufo, the voiceless retroflex stop /ʈ/ can become the voiced retroflex stop /ɖ/ in noun singularization when a syllabic nasal precedes it.

sources: Li, S. (2022). A Phonological Sketch of the Kufo Language. Open Research Repository.
Retroflex Sounds in Dravidian Languages - soniya. (2024).
Voiceless retroflex plosive | Laskon Wiki - Fandom.
Zou, K. (1990). ASSIMILATION AS SPREADING IN KOLAMI1. KU ScholarWorks.
